name: Singapore Satay (2 of 2)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine all marinade ingredients; add chicken. Stir to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
2. Soak bamboo skewers in water for at least 30 minutes.
3. In a medium saucepan, combine water, peanut butter, and garlic. Cook over medium heat until the mixture boils and thickens, stirring constantly.
4. Remove from heat and stir in the remaining peanut sauce ingredients. Cool to room temperature before serving.
5. Heat grill.
6. When ready to barbecue, drain chicken, reserving marinade. Thread 4 to 6 pieces of chicken on each skewer.
7. Place skewers on gas grill over low heat or on charcoal grill 4 to 6 inches from medium coals. Cook for 8 to 12 minutes, turning occasionally and brushing with reserved marinade.
8. Discard any remaining marinade.
9. Serve with peanut sauce. Yield: 16 to 24 appetizers.
WARNING: Ensure the chicken reaches a safe internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) before serving.
